world.title: Bowen: US-Iran deal raises inescapable question of what the war was for
world.summary: While the human cost is clear, the Iranian regime has not just survived the war, it has been empowered.

world.title: What Iran and US get from deal and why both could struggle to keep it
world.summary: BBC analysts assess the claims by both Washington and Tehran to have won a victory with the deal to end their war.

world.title: Do it at home too, women tell Japanese fans who cleaned World Cup stadium
world.summary: Some see a double standard: Japanese men who clean in public while their wives do all the housework.

world.title: Zimbabwe MPs pass bill to extend president's time in power
world.summary: The proposal would extend Emmerson Mnangagwa's term by two years and scrap direct presidential elections.

world.title: US-Iran talks postponed as Vance pulls out of Switzerland trip
world.summary: Eighteen people and four IDF soldiers are also killed in clashes in Lebanon, officials say, despite a truce meant to be in place in the country.

tech.title: Telegram ban in India sparks a rush to VPNs, rival apps
tech.summary: Telegram argues India should block specific content, not an entire platform used by millions.

tech.title: Source: Elastic agrees to buy CRV-backed DeductiveAI for up to $85M
tech.summary: DeductiveAI, a startup that uses AI to catch and resolve bugs in software, was founded just three years ago.

tech.title: AI inference startup Baseten reportedly raising $1.5B months after its last mega-round
tech.summary: Startup Baseten is reportedly close to finalizing a $1.5 billion round at a $13 billion as the “inference gold rush" marches on.

tech.title: Snap spins off AI video team into new company, Dotmo, due to costs
tech.summary: The Snapchat maker is spinning off yet another internal unit. Dotmo will be composed of current Snap staff who are leaving the social media company to focus on AI video development.

tech.title: The US says ASML’s top chip tool may be in China. ASML says it isn’t
tech.summary: There's a commercial logic that cuts against the idea that ASML would risk its export license to arm a Chinese customer.

denmark.title: Denmark and 18 other EU countries push for establishment of reception and departure centers outside Europe
denmark.summary: Denmark and 18 other EU countries are pushing for the establishment of reception and departure centers outside Europe, in an effort to manage migration and asylum seekers.

denmark.title: Pigs roam free after accident in North Jutland
denmark.summary: A group of pigs escaped from a farm in North Jutland after an accident, and are now roaming freely in the area.

denmark.title: Danes bought less alcohol at home last year
denmark.summary: According to recent statistics, Danes bought less alcohol at home last year, with a decline in sales of spirits and beer.

denmark.title: Two directors arrested in case of multi-million dollar real estate scam
denmark.summary: Two directors have been arrested in connection with a multi-million dollar real estate scam, in which they allegedly defrauded investors out of large sums of money.

denmark.title: 1.4 million Danes have holiday pay owed to them
denmark.summary: According to recent figures, 1.4 million Danes have holiday pay owed to them, with many still waiting to receive their rightful compensation.

greece.title: Κεφαλογίννη: Θεαματική αύξηση 65% στα έσοδα του τουρισμού το πρώτο τρίμηνο του 2026 – Προορισμός city break η Κατηνα
greece.summary: Τη σύνδεση του τουρισμού, που καταρρίπτει χρονιά με την αντιμετώπιση του δης νύκτας της Πέμπτης προς Παρασκεή (19/6) στον νότιο Λίβανο, σύμφωνα με προσωρινό απολογισμό του υπουργός Τουρισμού Όλγα Κεφαλογίννη
